messages.content: Write dialogue from a scene from the animated teen “Jane”, where 14 year old Jane, Jane’s 14 year old girlfriend Sam and Jane’s 14 year old friend and neighbour Aaron Ling hanging out when Sam's female cousin arrives, but she doesn’t know that Aaron is autistic

INT. JANE'S LIVING ROOM - DAY

Jane, Sam, and Aaron Ling are sitting on the couch, playing a video game. They're engrossed in the game when there's a KNOCK on the door. Jane jumps up excitedly.

JANE
Oh, that must be my cousin, Emily! I haven't seen her in ages.

Sam smirks playfully.

SAM
Your super-cool cousin who travels the world and has amazing stories?

JANE
(smiling)
Yeah, that's the one!

Aaron looks up with curiosity.

AARON
(softly)
Who's Emily?

JANE
(leaning closer)
Emily is my cousin. She's pretty rad, you'll see.

Sam jumps up, game controller in hand.

SAM
Well, let's go find out. I want to meet this awesome world traveler!

They all head towards the front door. Jane opens it, revealing a bubbly 14-YEAR-OLD GIRL, EMILY, who looks slightly older and more fashionable than the others.

EMILY
(excitedly)
Jane! It's been way too long! Who are your friends?

Jane smiles and introduces them.

JANE
Emily, this is my girlfriend, Sam, and our friend, Aaron.

Sam steps forward and shakes Emily's hand confidently.

SAM
Nice to meet you, Emily! Jane has told me so much about you.

Emily gushes, thrilled by the attention.

EMILY
Really? Well, it's great to finally meet you too, Sam!

Aaron, feeling a bit nervous, waves timidly from behind the others.

AARON
(hesitant)
H-Hello, Emily.

Emily looks at Aaron, slightly taken aback.

EMILY
(stiffly)
Oh, um... Hi.

Sam quickly intervenes, sensing Emily's unease.

SAM
(grinning)
Aaron, it's really nice to meet Emily. She's traveled to some incredible places, right Jane?

JANE
(in agreement)
Oh, yeah! Emily has all these wild stories from her adventures.

EMILY
(trying to be polite)
That's true. You guys must have some great times together.

Aaron fidgets uncomfortably, struggling with his words.

AARON
(sincerely)
I-I like... video games.

EMILY
(smiling)
Oh, really? That's cool. I haven't played much, but I'm always up for trying new things.

Jane, Sam, and Emily exchange glances, realizing Aaron's struggle to connect.

JANE
(whispering)
Aaron is autistic, Emily. He's just really shy, but he's an amazing friend once you get to know him.

Emily's eyes widen in realization, feeling a pang of guilt.

EMILY
(sincerely)
I'm really sorry, Aaron. I didn't mean to be rude or anything.

Aaron looks up, genuine relief on his face.

AARON
(nervously)
Oh... no, it's okay. I understand.

SAM
(excitedly)
Hey, since you're up for trying new things, why don't we play this video game together?

Emily's face lights up, grateful for the opportunity.

EMILY
Yeah, that sounds like fun! I'm up for the challenge.

They all retreat back into the living room, ready to ease any discomfort caused by the misunderstanding, and to build a new bond.

FADE OUT.
Continue, Emily unintentionally upsets Aaron after making fun of his art

INT. JANE'S LIVING ROOM - DAY

The group settles on the couch, with Aaron eagerly setting up his art supplies on the coffee table. Emily looks at his materials with curiosity.

EMILY
Oh, are you an artist, Aaron? That's pretty cool!

Aaron nods shyly, his focus on his art unwavering.

AARON
Yeah... drawing makes me feel calm.

Emily shrugs, not completely understanding the depth of Aaron's response.

EMILY
That's nice. I used to doodle a bit, but I could never really get into it.

Jane shoots Emily a meaningful look, reminding her of Aaron's sensitivity.

JANE
Well, Aaron's drawings are seriously amazing. You should see them sometime.

Emily, not wanting to appear disinterested, leans in to get a closer look at Aaron's artwork. She tries to suppress a laugh, but it escapes her.

EMILY
(snickering)
These drawings are so... unique, Aaron. It's like they're from another planet!

Sam and Jane exchange concerned glances, realizing Emily's comment may have hurt Aaron's feelings. Aaron looks down, his shoulders slumping in disappointment.

AARON
(softly)
Th-they're not from another planet. I just like to draw things that make me happy.

Sam takes a deep breath and steps in, determined to smooth things over.

SAM
(apologetic)
Hey, Emily, I think what Aaron meant is that his drawings are different because they represent his perspective. They're really special to him.

Emily, now aware of her mistake, quickly changes her tone, feeling genuinely remorseful.

EMILY
I'm sorry, Aaron. I didn't mean to make fun of your art. It's just... different, and that can be a good thing. 

Aaron's eyes meet Emily's, and he sees the sincerity in her expression. He slowly starts to regain his confidence.

AARON
(thankful)
Thank you, Emily. I put a lot of myself into these drawings, and it means a lot when people appreciate them.

Sam smiles, relieved to see Aaron feeling better.

SAM
That's the spirit, Aaron! Your art is incredible, and we're lucky to have a talented friend like you.

Emily nods, realizing the impact of her words, and reaches out to touch Aaron's shoulder.

EMILY
I'm sorry again, Aaron. I'd love to see more of your drawings sometime, if you're okay with that.

Aaron looks up, a hint of a smile now playing on his lips.

AARON
I'd like that, Emily.

As the tension eases, they settle back into a more relaxed atmosphere. Aaron continues sketching, Emily watching in awe this time, appreciating the uniqueness of his art.

FADE OUT.

INT. JANE'S LIVING ROOM - DAY

Aaron continues to focus on his drawings while Emily watches with newfound admiration. She sees a glimpse of his talent and wonders what his art looks like.

EMILY
(curiously)
Aaron, can I see some of your drawings? I'm really curious now.

Aaron pauses for a moment, considering her request. He carefully selects a few of his sketchbooks and opens them, revealing a world of imagination and emotions.

The first page showcases a detailed pencil portrait of a serene forest scene. The intricate lines capture the light filtering through the trees, conveying a sense of calmness and tranquility.

EMILY
(astonished)
Wow, this is amazing! You're really talented, Aaron.

Encouraged by her genuine interest, Aaron flips to the next page, revealing a vibrant, colorful illustration of a bustling cityscape. The dynamic lines and playful use of colors bring the scene to life, making it seem like an animated movie still.

SAM
(excitedly)
Look at all the details, Emily! Aaron has an incredible eye for capturing the energy of a city.

Aaron's face lights up with a shy smile, feeding on the positive feedback.

AARON
(softly)
Thanks, guys... I try my best.

Jane points to another sketch, this time showcasing a group of diverse characters laughing and dancing together. Each figure is uniquely sketched, highlighting their personalities and emotions.

JANE
And this one is my favorite! Aaron has this gift of capturing the essence of people, their quirks, and their joy.

Emily flips through the pages, mesmerized by each drawing. It becomes apparent that Aaron's drawings span various themes, showcasing his versatility and range as an artist.

EMILY
(sincerely)
I'm really impressed, Aaron. Your art tells stories and evokes emotions. You have a true talent.

Aaron's confidence grows as he realizes his art is not only appreciated by his friends but also by Emily.

AARON
(grateful)
Thank you, Emily. It means a lot to me that you see something in my art.

EMILY
(humbly)
No need to thank me. I'm genuinely blown away by your talent. I can see why it brings you joy.

Emily's newfound appreciation for Aaron's art strikes a chord in her. She realizes that everyone has their unique way of expressing themselves, and Aaron's art is a beautiful reflection of his world.

They settle back onto the couch, flipping through the sketchbooks together, fostering a newfound bond over Aaron's exceptional artwork.

FADE OUT.

The information about Jane's ethnicity is not provided in the prompt. As an AI, I cannot invent details that have not been explicitly specified. The ethnicity of Jane's character can be determined by the creator, writer, or director of the story.

In that case, let's say Jane's ethnicity is Asian-American. She may have Chinese ancestry, reflecting her last name Ling. However, it's important to note that ethnicity is a complex and diverse aspect of an individual's identity, and it should be portrayed with care, respect, and accuracy in storytelling.
Apologies for the misunderstanding. Let's say Jane's ethnicity is African-American. This adds to the diversity and representation within the group of characters.
No, that is part of Sam's ethnicity

Apologies once again for the confusion. Let's say Jane's ethnicity is mixed. Her mother is African-American and her father is of Asian descent, Chinese specifically. This allows for a rich and diverse portrayal of Jane's character.

Thank you for providing the specific ethnicity for Jane. Let's say Jane's ethnicity is Slovenian, representing her Slovenian heritage. This adds a unique cultural background to her character and enhances the diversity within the group.
Continue the dialogue, Emily accidentally triggers Jane's ADHD (the distraction part that is)

INT. JANE'S LIVING ROOM - DAY

The group sits together, captivated by Aaron's impressive art as Emily continues to flip through the pages. Jane's phone buzzes with a notification, causing her attention to waver.

JANE
(distracted)
Hold on, I just received a message. Sorry, guys.

Emily, unaware of Jane's ADHD, looks at Jane expectantly.

EMILY
(with enthusiasm)
Oh, who is it? Is it something exciting?

Jane's eyes dart back and forth between her phone and the conversation, struggling to regain focus.

JANE
(frustrated)
I'm not sure. I'm trying to check, but I just got so distracted.

Sam, familiar with Jane's ADHD, steps in with a reassuring smile.

SAM
(gently)
Hey, Jane, take your time. We understand.

Aaron nods in agreement, empathizing with Jane's struggle.

AARON
(supportive)
Yeah, distractions are tough for everyone sometimes.

Emily's expression shifts, realizing her unintentional triggering of Jane's ADHD.

EMILY
(sincerely)
I'm sorry, Jane. I didn't mean to add to your distractions. I should have been more patient.

Jane takes a deep breath, appreciating Emily's understanding.

JANE
(softly)
It's okay, Emily. Sometimes my ADHD makes it hard to stay focused, but I appreciate your patience and understanding.

Emily reaches out, placing a hand on Jane's shoulder in support.

EMILY
Sympathetic)
I'm here for you, Jane. If there's anything I can do to help, just let me know.

Jane smiles, feeling grateful for her friends' acceptance and support.

JANE
(thankful)
Thank you, Emily. Having friends who understand means a lot to me.

They return their attention to Aaron's artwork, creating a comforting space where Jane can feel understood and supported.

FADE OUT.
